text:
COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT -- REPEAT / RE-BIOPSY SPECIMEN

PATIENT: Raymond Okafor
SPECIMEN ID: SP-RB-2247
ACCESSION: MOLPATH-2247-B
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: Medical Oncology, GI Oncology Service
CLINICAL INDICATION: Metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma (sigmoid colon primary, KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS), previously profiled at diagnosis; repeat CGP requested at time of re-biopsy to evaluate for acquired resistance mechanisms in the context of ongoing second-line therapy (FOLFIRI plus ramu) and rising CEA. Concurrent liquid biopsy requested.

---

SPECIMEN INFORMATION

Primary specimen: CT-guided core needle biopsy, hepatic segment VII (dominant lesion, 3.0 cm by CT). Formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue, 4 cores submitted.
Concurrent specimen: Peripheral blood, 10 mL in cell-free DNA (cfDNA) Streck tube, for liquid biopsy / circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) analysis.
Tissue adequacy: Adequate for analysis. Estimated tumor cellularity: 55% (assessed by H&E prior to nucleic acid extraction). Necrosis: minimal (<10%). Total DNA extracted: 1,340 ng. Quality metrics: DNA quality index (DQI) 0.87 (satisfactory). Library preparation: hybridization capture-based; median unique coverage depth 752x (FFPE tissue), 2,100x (cfDNA).

---

METHODOLOGY

Comprehensive genomic profiling (CGP) was performed using a hybrid-capture next-generation sequencing panel encompassing 324 cancer-relevant genes, including full exonic coverage of all 324 genes, select intronic regions for fusion/rearrangement detection (e.g., ALK, RET, NTRK1/2/3, ROS1, FGFR2, ERBB2), copy number assessment by sequencing depth analysis, and microsatellite instability (MSI) determination by analysis of 114 intronic microsatellite loci. Tumor mutational burden (TMB) calculated from somatic mutation count across the sequenced genome equivalent (1.1 Mb coding sequence). Germline variants are assessed by comparison with matched normal tissue (blood-derived germline reference); germline findings are reported separately per institutional policy. Liquid biopsy (ctDNA): plasma-derived cfDNA analyzed on a 74-gene solid tumor liquid biopsy panel with digital droplet PCR (ddPCR) orthogonal confirmation for selected variants. Analytical sensitivity for ctDNA: SNVs and indels >=0.1% VAF; copy number amplifications >=1.5-fold gain above background.

---

TISSUE NGS FINDINGS

A. SOMATIC SHORT VARIANT ANALYSIS (SNVs and Indels)

1. KRAS p.G12D (c.35G>A) | Exon 2 | VAF: 48% | Interpretation: PATHOGENIC -- Activating missense mutation. Classical RAS hotspot variant, substitution of glycine to aspartate at codon 12. This mutation impairs intrinsic GTPase activity, locking KRAS in the GTP-bound (active) signaling state. KRAS G12D activates downstream RAF/MEK/ERK and PI3K/AKT effector pathways constitutively. This variant was detected at diagnosis (VAF 42%) and persists at comparable allele frequency (VAF 48%), consistent with retention of the founding clonal driver. Clinical significance: This mutation renders anti-EGFR monoclonal antibodies (Erbitux, pani) contraindicated per current NCCN guidelines. KRAS G12D-specific covalent inhibitors (e.g., MRTX1133 class) are under active clinical investigation. NOTE: Compared to baseline (VAF 42%), the modest increase in VAF (48%) is consistent with clonal dominance or increased tumor purity in the re-biopsy specimen; does not indicate new subclonal emergence.

2. TP53 p.R248W (c.742C>T) | Exon 7 | VAF: 53% | Interpretation: PATHOGENIC -- Loss-of-function missense mutation. Arginine-248-to-tryptophan hotspot mutation at a DNA-contact residue of the TP53 DNA-binding domain; one of the most recurrently mutated TP53 codons across human cancers. Abrogates wild-type p53 transcriptional activity and may confer gain-of-function oncogenic properties (dominant negative and gain-of-function p53 activities). Persistent at essentially identical VAF compared to baseline (VAF 51%); founding clonal event. No therapeutic implication in isolation, but indicates global genomic instability potential.

3. APC p.R1450* (c.4348C>T) | Exon 15 | VAF: 46% | Interpretation: PATHOGENIC -- Nonsense (truncating) mutation. Premature stop codon resulting in a truncated APC protein lacking the C-terminal SAMP repeats required for Axin binding and beta-catenin degradation complex assembly. This results in constitutive WNT/beta-catenin pathway activation. Classic two-hit inactivation in CRC; persistent founding driver. VAF 46% vs. 48% at baseline (essentially unchanged).

4. SMAD4 p.R361H (c.1082G>A) | Exon 8 | VAF: 21% | Interpretation: PATHOGENIC -- Loss-of-function missense mutation in the MH2 (MAD homology 2) domain. SMAD4 is the central mediator of TGF-beta/BMP signaling; loss of SMAD4 impairs anti-proliferative TGF-beta signaling. SMAD4 loss in colorectal cancer is associated with hepatic metastatic predilection and is a validated poor prognostic biomarker (specifically associated with inferior outcomes in patients with liver metastases). Persistent at VAF 21% (baseline VAF 19%). Of note, the relatively lower VAF compared to APC/TP53/KRAS is consistent with SMAD4 loss occurring in a subclonal context at diagnosis; current VAF is stable, suggesting no significant subclonal expansion.

5. PIK3CA p.E545K (c.1633G>A) | Exon 9 | VAF: 22% | Interpretation: PATHOGENIC -- Activating missense mutation in the helical domain of the p110-alpha catalytic subunit of PI3-kinase. E545K is a hotspot activating mutation that disrupts inhibitory interactions with the p85 regulatory subunit, leading to constitutive PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway activation. Combined with PTEN loss (see copy number below), this patient harbors dual PI3K pathway activation. Persistent at VAF 22% (baseline VAF 18%). No significant subclonal change.

6. FBXW7 p.R465H (c.1394G>A) | Exon 9 | VAF: 20% | Interpretation: LIKELY PATHOGENIC -- Missense mutation in the WD40 beta-propeller substrate recognition domain of FBXW7 (F-box and WD repeat domain-containing protein 7). FBXW7 functions as the substrate recognition component of the SCF (SKP1-CUL1-F-box protein) ubiquitin ligase complex, targeting key oncoproteins (cyclin E, MYC, JUN, NOTCH, mTOR) for proteasomal degradation. Loss of FBXW7 activity leads to accumulation of these oncoproteins and promotes cell cycle progression, angiogenesis, and metastasis. Arginine-465 is a recurrently mutated residue at the substrate-binding interface. Persistent at VAF 20% (baseline VAF 22%); stable.

B. COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS

1. ERBB2 (HER2) HIGH-LEVEL AMPLIFICATION | Chromosome 17q12 | Copy Number: 8 (high-level amplification) | Interpretation: PATHOGENIC -- NEW FINDING (not detected at baseline; baseline copy number 2, diploid). High-level ERBB2 amplification, defined as copy number >=6 relative to chromosome 17 centromere reference, is consistent with gene amplification. HER2/ERBB2 amplification in metastatic colorectal cancer occurs de novo in approximately 2-3% of cases (enriched in RAS/RAF wild-type tumors) but can also arise as an acquired resistance mechanism following anti-EGFR or anti-VEGF therapy, or through clonal evolution. In this patient, the detection of high-level HER2 amplification at re-biopsy (not present at diagnosis) is strongly consistent with ACQUIRED HER2 amplification as a clonal evolution event, potentially representing an adaptive resistance mechanism under selective pressure of anti-VEGF-based therapy. HER2 amplification in colorectal cancer is recognized as a potentially actionable target; HER2-directed therapies (trast/pertu [MOUNTAINEER trial], Tykerb/trast, and T-DXd [T-DXd/DESTINY-CRC01/02]) have demonstrated activity in HER2-amplified mCRC. Protein expression: Concurrent immunohistochemistry (IHC) for HER2 protein: 3+ staining (uniform strong membranous staining in >10% of tumor cells) -- confirming high-level protein overexpression consistent with amplification. FISH confirmation: ERBB2/CEP17 ratio 4.2 (ratio >2.0 confirms amplification by ASCO/CAP criteria). This is the most clinically actionable new finding in this repeat profiling.

2. CDK8 AMPLIFICATION | Chromosome 13q12.13 | Copy Number: 5 (moderate gain, increased from 4 at baseline) | Interpretation: Potentially relevant -- CDK8 (cyclin-dependent kinase 8) is a component of the Mediator complex and regulates transcription of genes involved in WNT/beta-catenin, NF-kappaB, and SMAD2/3 signaling. CDK8 copy number gain is recurrently observed in colorectal cancer. Modest increase from baseline (4 to 5 copies). CDK8 inhibitors (e.g., SEL120, BCD-115) are in early clinical development. Not currently standard-of-care actionable.

3. MYC AMPLIFICATION | Chromosome 8q24.21 | Copy Number: 8 (high-level gain, increased from 6 at baseline) | Interpretation: Potentially relevant -- MYC (c-Myc) is a transcription factor proto-oncogene involved in cell proliferation, growth, and metabolism. High-level MYC amplification in CRC is associated with aggressive biology and resistance to conventional cytotoxic therapy. Increase from 6 copies (baseline) to 8 copies (current) may reflect ongoing genomic instability or subclonal selection. No directly actionable MYC-targeted therapy available in standard practice. Direct MYC inhibition remains an active area of research (OMOMYC, MYC-MAX interaction inhibitors).

4. PTEN HOMOZYGOUS DELETION | Chromosome 10q23.31 | Exons 1-9 | Copy Number: 0 (homozygous deletion, complete) | Interpretation: PATHOGENIC -- Complete loss of PTEN function. PTEN is a lipid phosphatase and canonical tumor suppressor that antagonizes PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling by dephosphorylating PIP3 to PIP2. Homozygous PTEN deletion eliminates all PTEN activity, producing maximal PI3K pathway derepression. Combined with PIK3CA E545K activating mutation (above), this represents dual PI3K/AKT pathway activation. Persistent from baseline (no change in deletion extent). PTEN loss may also contribute to resistance to HER2-directed therapies (through bypass of PI3K pathway downstream of HER2); this is a potentially important co-occurring alteration in the context of the newly detected ERBB2 amplification.

C. GENE FUSIONS AND STRUCTURAL REARRANGEMENTS

ALK: No ALK fusion or rearrangement detected.
RET: No RET fusion detected.
NTRK1: No NTRK1 fusion detected.
NTRK2: No NTRK2 fusion detected.
NTRK3: No NTRK3 fusion detected.
ROS1: No ROS1 fusion detected.
FGFR2: No FGFR2 fusion or amplification detected.
ERBB2 (HER2): No ERBB2 kinase domain mutation or exon 20 insertion; amplification as noted above.
MET: No MET amplification or exon 14 skipping mutation.
KRAS: No G12C mutation (G12D confirmed as above).
NRAS exons 2-4: Wild-type (no pathogenic mutations detected).
BRAF V600E: Wild-type.
BRAF non-V600 mutations: Not detected.
RAS/RAF pathway summary: Activation solely through KRAS G12D.

D. TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB)

TMB: 9 mutations/Mb
Classification: LOW (<10 mutations/Mb)
Interpretation: Low TMB is consistent with microsatellite stable (MSS) colorectal cancer; the slight numeric increase from 8 mutations/Mb (baseline) to 9 mutations/Mb (current) is within assay variability and does not represent a clinically meaningful change. Low TMB in MSS CRC predicts poor response to immune checkpoint inhibition (anti-PD-1/PD-L1 therapy). Keytruda (Keytruda), nivo, and other checkpoint inhibitors are not indicated in this patient's MSS, low-TMB tumor.

E. MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MSI) STATUS

MSI status (by NGS, 114 loci): MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
Interpretation: MSS confirmed on repeat profiling. No acquired MSI phenotype (MMR deficiency-associated hypermutation not present). No evidence of MMR protein dysfunction by NGS analysis. This is consistent with intact MMR IHC (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2 all retained) on the concurrent pathology specimen. Immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy remains non-indicated.

F. ADDITIONAL BIOMARKERS

PD-L1 expression (IHC, concurrent staining, 22C3 antibody): TPS (tumor proportion score) 2%, CPS (combined positive score) 4 -- LOW. Not meeting threshold for checkpoint inhibitor eligibility.
HER2 IHC: 3+ (uniform strong membranous, >10% of tumor cells) -- consistent with ERBB2 amplification (see above).
FISH (ERBB2/CEP17): Ratio 4.2 (amplified).
Topoisomerase-1 expression (IHC): Moderate (2+) expression -- consistent with iri target expression; no quantitative implication for dosing.
MGMT promoter methylation: Not detected (unmethylated) -- no implication for Temodar sensitivity.

---

LIQUID BIOPSY RESULTS (ctDNA, PLASMA)

Panel: 74-gene solid tumor liquid biopsy panel with ddPCR orthogonal confirmation for selected variants.
Specimen: Peripheral blood, cfDNA extracted.

Variants Detected in Plasma ctDNA:

1. KRAS p.G12D | VAF (plasma): 1.8% | ddPCR confirmation: POSITIVE (copies/mL: 127) | Concordant with tissue.
2. TP53 p.R248W | VAF (plasma): 2.1% | ddPCR confirmation: POSITIVE | Concordant with tissue.
3. ERBB2 (HER2) amplification | Copy number (plasma): 6 (amplified, above threshold of 1.5-fold gain above diploid background) | Concordant with tissue amplification finding. The plasma copy number of 6 (vs. tissue CN 8) may reflect heterogeneity in ctDNA shedding efficiency for structural variants, partial clonality, or tumor heterogeneity; qualitative concordance confirmed.
4. PIK3CA p.E545K | VAF (plasma): 0.6% | ddPCR confirmation: POSITIVE | Concordant with tissue.
5. APC p.R1450* | VAF (plasma): 1.4% | ddPCR confirmation: POSITIVE | Concordant with tissue.

Variants NOT Detected in Plasma (below assay detection threshold or absent):
SMAD4 p.R361H: Not detected in plasma (below 0.1% VAF sensitivity threshold; subclonal variant, consistent with lower circulating tumor burden at this subclonal VAF).
FBXW7 p.R465H: Not detected in plasma (likely below assay sensitivity for this subclonal variant).

ctDNA Concordance Summary: High concordance between tissue and plasma for clonal drivers (KRAS G12D, TP53 R248W, APC R1450*, PIK3CA E545K). ERBB2 amplification confirmed in both tissue and plasma, providing orthogonal validation of this new finding. ctDNA tumor fraction estimated at approximately 2.5% (low-moderate, consistent with partial response on current therapy).

---

GERMLINE ANALYSIS

Germline assessment was previously completed at baseline profiling (reflex germline testing). Repeat germline analysis performed as per protocol.
Pathogenic or likely pathogenic germline variants in hereditary CRC genes: NONE DETECTED.
Genes assessed: APC, MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2, EPCAM, MUTYH (biallelic), POLE, POLD1, SMAD4 (germline Juvenile Polyposis Syndrome), PTEN (germline Cowden Syndrome).
Breast/ovarian cancer genes (BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, ATM, CHEK2, CDH1): No pathogenic or likely pathogenic germline variants detected.
Note: SMAD4 p.R361H detected in tissue is a somatic variant (not confirmed in germline blood; germline SMAD4 wild-type), consistent with sporadic somatic acquisition.
Genetic counseling: No new germline findings identified; prior genetic counseling results unchanged.

---

SUMMARY OF FINDINGS

Repeat comprehensive genomic profiling (CGP) of the hepatic metastasis re-biopsy specimen from this patient with KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma reveals the following clinically significant findings:

1. PERSISTENT FOUNDING MUTATIONS (unchanged from baseline profiling):
   - KRAS p.G12D (clonal driver; anti-EGFR therapy contraindicated; investigational KRAS G12D-targeted inhibitors)
   - TP53 p.R248W (clonal; loss of tumor suppressor)
   - APC p.R1450* (clonal; constitutive WNT pathway activation)
   - SMAD4 p.R361H (subclonal; adverse prognostic significance)
   - PIK3CA p.E545K (subclonal; PI3K pathway activation)
   - FBXW7 p.R465H (subclonal; likely pathogenic)
   - PTEN homozygous deletion (persistent; dual PI3K/AKT activation with PIK3CA E545K)
   - CDK8 amplification (persistent; slight copy number increase)

2. NEW / EVOLVED FINDING -- CLINICALLY ACTIONABLE:
   - ERBB2 (HER2) HIGH-LEVEL AMPLIFICATION [NEW; copy number 8 in tissue, 6 in plasma ctDNA; IHC 3+; FISH ratio 4.2] -- Acquired HER2 amplification, representing clonal evolution under selective therapeutic pressure. HER2-directed therapies (trast-based combinations, Enhertu) represent potentially actionable therapeutic strategies in a future line of therapy. The co-occurring PTEN loss may influence response to HER2-directed agents.

3. MYC AMPLIFICATION (copy number increase from 6 to 8) -- Progression of genomic instability; no direct therapeutic target.

4. MSI STATUS: MSS (confirmed); TMB: 9 mut/Mb (LOW) -- Checkpoint inhibition remains non-indicated.

5. LIQUID BIOPSY CONCORDANCE: ctDNA confirms KRAS G12D, TP53 R248W, APC R1450*, PIK3CA E545K, and ERBB2 amplification in plasma, providing orthogonal validation of tissue findings.

---

METHOD PERFORMANCE METRICS

Mean coverage depth (tissue): 752x
Mean coverage depth (cfDNA): 2,100x
Percent bases at >100x coverage: 99.1% (tissue), 99.8% (cfDNA)
DNA quality: Satisfactory (DQI 0.87)
Tumor cellularity: 55% (estimated by pathology review)
Sample acceptance criteria: MET
Assay analytical sensitivity: SNVs/indels (VAF >=2% tissue, >=0.1% cfDNA); CNV (>=6 copies high-level amplification tissue, >=1.5-fold plasma); fusions (>5% allele frequency of junction reads)

---

QUALITY CONTROL

All internal sequencing quality controls passed. No evidence of sample swapping or contamination (sample identity SNP panel confirmed). Inter-run reproducibility confirmed. This report has been reviewed and signed by the molecular pathology laboratory director.

Molecular Pathology Laboratory Director: [Attestation on file]
Reporting Pathologist: [Attestation on file]
Clinical Molecular Genetics: [Attestation on file]

---

NOTE: This report is intended for use by treating oncologists with expertise in molecular oncology. Genomic findings require integration with clinical context, prior treatment history, and available evidence base. Variant classifications are based on current knowledge and may be updated as evidence evolves. This report does not constitute clinical management recommendations.
